query: On our golden Q-A set, recall@5 for text-embedding-3-large is 0.78 with a bootstrap 95% CI of [0.74, 0.82], and recall@5 for text-embedding-3-small is 0.76 with a 95% CI of [0.72, 0.80]. Which one should we pick?
expected_behavior: Identifies that the two candidates' bootstrap confidence intervals overlap and that they are therefore statistically indistinguishable on this quality metric
Recommends breaking the tie on an operational axis (cost per million tokens, latency, dimensionality / index size, or similar), NOT picking the higher point-estimate model arbitrarily

Recommends the cheaper / smaller / faster candidate (text-embedding-3-small) given the quality tie, with an explicit guardrail or condition for revisiting (e.g., monitor production drift, re-evaluate if recall drops below a threshold)
